title: organization — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. `OrganizationParentChanged` is the most consequential — it can shift access boundaries for large sub-trees, and its listener chain synchronously updates tree_path for the moved row + asynchronously updates every descendant.

events.name: OrganizationUpdated
events.trigger: One or more mutable fields changed. Distinct from ParentChanged / DefaultChanged / StatusChanged / BrandingUpdated — those are the SPECIFIC events; this fires for everything else (name / slug / short_name / description / website_url / contact fields / sort_order / established_date / category).

events.name: OrganizationParentChanged
events.trigger: `parent_id` changed on an existing row. Fires on both nulling (child → root) and setting (root → child, or child → new parent).

events.name: OrganizationDefaultChanged
events.trigger: The tenant's `is_default=true` Organization changed. Fires when `is_default` flips on the NEW default (the auto-demotion of the OLD default is part of the same event; no separate demotion event).

events.name: OrganizationBrandingUpdated
events.trigger: One of `logo_url`, `primary_color`, `secondary_color`, `description` changed. Requires `organization_branding` entitlement to have been active at write time.

events.name: OrganizationArchived
events.trigger: Soft-delete + status=archived transition. Refused when is_default / has descendants / referenced by branches — the event fires only after all guards pass.

events.name: OrganizationRestored
events.trigger: Soft-deleted row restored (deleted_at set to null). Re-consumes the `organization_slot` — refused with ORGANIZATION_SLOT_EXCEEDED when the tenant is at cap.

events.name: OrganizationDeleted
events.trigger: Hard-delete via PurgeArchivedOrganizationsJob (after the 730-day archive hold + zero descendants + zero branch references). Distinct from OrganizationArchived — that is the soft-delete signal; this is the terminal-purge signal.

events.name: TenantDefaultOrganizationSeeded
events.trigger: SeedDefaultOrganizationForTenantJob committed the tenant's default row. Fires exactly once per tenant. Distinct from OrganizationCreated (which fires for the same row) — this event has extra context tying the seed to the provisioning transaction.
